page: Amazon Web Services m8i.24xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 96 vCPUs derived from 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 384 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4 GB of RAM per core. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. Notably, this instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The server achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, as well as Passmark CPU and database operation benchmarks. Memory performance is highly competitive, with read-only small block and large block memory bandwidth placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The processor's advanced instruction support, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), drives top-tier results in ray tracing, software compilation, and cryptography tests.

This hardware profile is configured for enterprise-grade general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded database operations, and static web serving. It also demonstrates strong capabilities for CPU-based machine learning inference, particularly for prompt processing on medium and large language models. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, and the absence of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy graphics rendering or deep learning training workloads. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high resource density and advanced instruction sets deliver strong performance per resource for compute-heavy and memory-intensive enterprise applications without requiring specialized GPU or high-memory instances.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.24xlarge is a general-purpose server instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores. It is configured with 384 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, delivering a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides 40 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations, aided by hardware-accelerated instructions like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This instance is optimized for multi-threaded enterprise applications, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.24xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and multi-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.24xlarge is an Intel-based general-purpose server with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose virtual server featuring ninety-six Intel Xeon vCPUs and DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with dedicated allocation.
Equipped with 384 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory.
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU benchmark performance.
Features advanced hardware acceleration including AVX-512 and Intel AMX.
Provides 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor.
Optimized for enterprise databases, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.
